title: SolarWinds Web Help Desk Third-Generation Deserialization RCE Actively Exploited: CISA KEV Mandates Federal Remediation by March 23 (CVE-2025-26399)
affected_products: SolarWinds Web Help Desk 12.8.7 and all prior versions

sources.name: Huntress - Active Exploitation of SolarWinds Web Help Desk CVE-2025-26399
Qualys ThreatPROTECT - SolarWinds Web Help Desk RCE CVE-2025-26399
Horizon3.ai - CVE-2025-40551: Another SolarWinds Web Help Desk Deserialization Issue
Rapid7 - Multiple Critical SolarWinds Web Help Desk Vulnerabilities
watchTowr Labs - SolarWinds Web Help Desk Pre-Auth RCE Chain
The Hacker News - SolarWinds Releases Hotfix for Critical Web Help Desk Bug
BleepingComputer - SolarWinds Releases Third Patch to Fix Web Help Desk RCE Bug
Microsoft Security Blog - Active Exploitation of SolarWinds Web Help Desk

timeline.event: CVE-2024-28986 disclosed: original AjaxProxy deserialization RCE in SolarWinds Web Help Desk; hotfix released
CVE-2024-28988 disclosed: bypass of first patch; SolarWinds issues second hotfix
CVE-2025-26399 disclosed: third-generation bypass of AjaxProxy deserialization fix; SolarWinds releases Web Help Desk 12.8.7 Hotfix 1
watchTowr Labs documents pre-auth RCE chain; earliest confirmed exploitation evidence observed
SolarWinds discloses six additional critical/high vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-40551 through CVE-2025-40554)
Microsoft Security publishes detailed analysis of active exploitation campaign targeting SolarWinds Web Help Desk
Huntress SOC documents active exploitation case with full attack chain: RCE → Zoho ManageEngine RMM → Velociraptor → DCSync
CISA adds CVE-2025-26399 to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog; federal agencies given March 23, 2026 remediation deadline

simple_summary.what_happened: SolarWinds Web Help Desk, a popular IT service management platform used by thousands of enterprises, contains a critical vulnerability that allows attackers to run malicious code on affected servers without needing any login credentials. This is the third version of the same fundamental flaw — attackers have bypassed two previous patches, and exploitation is now confirmed and widespread. CISA added this to its catalog of actively exploited vulnerabilities on March 9, 2026, with a federal deadline of March 23.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: Organizations running SolarWinds Web Help Desk version 12.8.7 or earlier, including government agencies, healthcare institutions, financial organizations, and enterprises using WHD as their IT helpdesk platform. The product is commonly internet-exposed to allow customer support ticket submission.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: Attackers scan the internet for SolarWinds Web Help Desk instances running version 12.8.7 or earlier with the /AjaxProxy endpoint exposed. Step 2: They craft a malicious serialized Java object payload and send it to the vulnerable /AjaxProxy endpoint without any authentication. Step 3: The server deserializes the malicious object, triggering a gadget chain that executes arbitrary operating system commands with SYSTEM-level privileges. Step 4: Attackers silently install Zoho ManageEngine Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) agent to establish persistent remote access, often downloading the MSI from file-hosting services like Catbox.moe. Step 5: Attackers deploy Velociraptor as a secondary C2 channel via Supabase storage buckets, then conduct Active Directory reconnaissance using domain enumeration commands. Step 6: Attackers use DLL sideloading techniques against wab.exe to access LSASS memory and extract credentials, then perform DCSync attacks to compromise the entire domain.
what_you_should_do: Immediately update SolarWinds Web Help Desk to version 2026.1 (comprehensive fix) or apply 12.8.7 Hotfix 1 as a minimum emergency measure
Restrict the /AjaxProxy endpoint from public internet access and place WHD behind a VPN or network access control
Search for unauthorized remote management tools: Zoho ManageEngine RMM, Velociraptor, unexpected scheduled tasks, and suspicious MSI installation history
Rotate all service account credentials and administrative passwords for WHD and any systems the server can access
Review domain controller logs for DCSync activity and unauthorized LSASS access events
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: This is a no-authentication, low-complexity, high-impact exploit against a system that organizations deliberately expose to the internet for customer support. Attackers gain SYSTEM-level access immediately upon exploitation, then use that foothold for full Active Directory domain compromise via credential theft and DCSync attacks. The three-times-bypassed patch history shows this flaw is deeply embedded in the product architecture. CISA's March 23 deadline and active federal exploitation evidence mean any unpatched instance is likely already compromised or targeted.

tactics.description: Attackers exploit the unauthenticated deserialization flaw in the /AjaxProxy endpoint of internet-facing SolarWinds Web Help Desk instances
Deserialization gadget chain executes arbitrary OS commands via cmd.exe spawned from java.exe under the WHD wrapper.exe service process
Attackers silently install Zoho ManageEngine RMM agent and Velociraptor as dual persistent remote access channels
Attackers abuse wab.exe combined with a malicious sspicli.dll to access LSASS memory without triggering standard credential-dumping detections
LSASS memory is accessed via DLL sideloading to harvest plaintext credentials and hashes from the compromised host
Attackers enumerate domain accounts using net group 'domain computers' /do and similar Active Directory queries
Stolen high-privilege credentials are used to perform DCSync attacks against domain controllers, replicating all domain credential hashes

detection_guidance.summary: Focus detection on the /AjaxProxy HTTP endpoint for serialized Java object payloads (magic bytes aced0005), anomalous process chains originating from wrapper.exe and java.exe, installation of unauthorized RMM tools, and DCSync activity from non-domain-controller hosts.

log_sources.description: Monitor HTTP POST requests to /AjaxProxy for Java serialization magic bytes (aced0005 in hex) and oversized request bodies
Monitor Event ID 4104 (PowerShell script block logging), 4688 (process creation), and 4662 (DCSync - replication access on domain controllers)
Monitor for wrapper.exe → java.exe → cmd.exe process chains and MSI silent installation execution
Monitor for outbound connections from WHD server to Catbox.moe, Supabase CDN, Zoho ManageEngine infrastructure, and Cloudflare tunnel endpoints

what_to_look_for.indicator: HTTP POST to /AjaxProxy with Java serialization header
what_to_look_for.description: Serialized Java objects begin with the magic bytes 0xACED0005 — presence in /AjaxProxy request body indicates deserialization exploit attempt

what_to_look_for.indicator: Anomalous process chain: wrapper.exe → java.exe → cmd.exe
what_to_look_for.description: The WHD service (wrapper.exe) should not spawn cmd.exe; any such chain indicates post-exploitation command execution
Attackers silently install Zoho ManageEngine from external MSI hosted on Catbox.moe; check for unexpected Zoho processes and files
MITRE T1003.006 - replication request from a non-DC host indicates credential harvesting via DCSync using stolen privileges
Velociraptor is a legitimate DFIR tool being weaponized as C2; unexpected execution from non-standard paths is highly suspicious

immediate_actions.action: Apply emergency patch or upgrade SolarWinds Web Help Desk
immediate_actions.description: Update to Web Help Desk 2026.1 (full fix) or apply 12.8.7 Hotfix 1 immediately. Verify patch by checking HikariCP.jar, whd-core.jar, whd-web.jar, and whd-persistence.jar modification timestamps in the WHD /lib directory.

Immediately remove direct public internet access to the SolarWinds Web Help Desk server or apply WAF rules blocking requests to /AjaxProxy with serialized Java content. Place WHD behind VPN for all administrative access.

immediate_actions.action: Hunt for unauthorized RMM tools and persistence mechanisms
immediate_actions.description: Search all WHD servers for Zoho ManageEngine RMM agent files, Velociraptor binaries, unexpected scheduled tasks, and recently created services. Check Windows Add/Remove Programs for unauthorized MSI installations from January 2026 onwards.
immediate_actions.command: Get-WmiObject Win32_Product | Where-Object { $_.InstallDate -ge '20260116' } | Select Name, InstallDate, Vendor

immediate_actions.action: Rotate credentials and investigate for DCSync activity
immediate_actions.description: Immediately rotate service account passwords for WHD and all accounts the server could access. Review Security event logs on domain controllers for Event ID 4662 with replication GUIDs from non-DC hosts, indicating potential DCSync attacks.

immediate_actions.action: Engage incident response if exploitation indicators found
immediate_actions.description: If any RMM tools, Velociraptor, or DCSync evidence is found, treat the system as fully compromised. Isolate from network, preserve forensic evidence, and conduct full Active Directory password reset for all accounts including krbtgt (twice).
